response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced senior full-stack software engineer with 25+ years across web, mobile, and some low-latency backend systems, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires SoC and IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU/accelerator architecture knowledge, and deep-learning workload performance analysis — none of which appear in his employment history or projects. His performance experience is at the application/services layer (async I/O, caching in DLang), not at the silicon or accelerator level.
strengths: Extensive engineering experience (25+ years) with clear seniority and breadth across stacks
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components, wave field synthesis thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from performance-architecture domain
